_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Only `blank-blog-template.md` currently has an explicit slug. Most draft files lack one entirely.

Hugo's `:slugorcontentbasename` may preserve the `__` prefix in permalinks. This could be a new convention or a rename of existing drafts.

The plan is to rename drafts to `__filename.md` and add `slug: "original-name"` to their frontmatter. This preserves existing URLs when the post is later published.

Need to verify Hugo's handling of double-underscore filenames and whether it ignores underscore-prefixed files. Should also check internal links to drafts. The request might be a skill-update rule rather than a bulk rename.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Checking Hugo behavior with `__`-prefixed filenames and scanning for internal links to draft posts.

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Hugo isn't installed in the sandbox, but it processes files starting with underscores.

Hugo ignores all files prefixed with `_`. Only `_index.md` creates a section page. Regular files starting with `_` would be skipped.

The `__` prefix would cause Hugo to ignore the draft files entirely. A different prefix such as `draft-` or `_draft-` may be needed instead.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="web_search"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **web_search**</summary>

```json
{
  "searchTerm": "Hugo content filesEnvironments files starting with underscore prefix"

```
Result:

```json
{
  "isFinal": true,
  "references": [
    {
      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [Project Structure | Ursula Hackett](https://ursulahackett.com/courses/hugo-blox/guide/project-structure/)\n2. [Can _partials be page-path specific too? - support - HUGO](https://discourse.gohugo.io/t/can-partials-be-page-path-specific-too/54836)\n3. [Add a more flexible filename identifier scheme that also allows setting roles and versions · Issue #14750 · gohugoio/hugo](https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/issues/14750)\n4. [Data Templates | Hugo](https://remove-version-from-svg-example--gohugoio.netlify.app/templates/data-templates/)\n5. [Front matter](https://gohugo.io/content-management/front-matter/)\n\nSynthesis:\nIn Hugo, files starting with an underscore are treated as special components or reserved entities. Specifically, the home page and section listing pages must be named _index.md [1]. Folders in the layouts directory that do not start with an underscore represent the root of a page path, while folders starting with an underscore are treated differently, such as for partials, shortcodes, or markup [2]. Additionally, content filenames can use a wrapper syntax with underscores—such as mypage._myidentifier_.md—to define opaque identifiers that Hugo uses to manage content dimensions like language, version, or role [3].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eProject Structure | Ursula Hackett\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://ursulahackett.com/courses/hugo-blox/guide/project-structure/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e`content/` for your Markdown-formatted content files (homepage, etc.)\n[...]\n- `_index.md` the homepage (Hugo requires that the homepage and archive pages have an underscore prefix)\n[...]\nHugo gives us two options to name standard page files: as`TITLE/index.md` or`TITLE.md` where`TITLE` is your page name.\n[...]\nThe homepage is a special case as Hugo requires the homepage and listing pages to be named`_index.md`.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003
… (output truncated)
```

{
  "markdown": "Content organization Content organization | Hugo\n\nMenu\n\nSearch\n\nGitHub 88629 stars Mastodon\n\nHugo Documentation\n\nHugo assumes that the same structure that works to organize your source content is used to organize the rendered site.\n\n## Page bundles\n\nHugo supports page-relative images and other resources packaged into`Page Bundles`.\n\nThese terms are connected, and you also need to read about page resources and image processing to get the full picture.\n\n```\ncontent/\n├── blog/\n│   ├── hugo-is-cool/\n│   │   ├── images/\n│   │   │   ├── funnier-cat.jpg\n│   │   │   └── funny-cat.jpg\n│   │   ├── cats-info.md\n│   │   └── index.md\n│   ├── posts/\n│   │   ├── post1.md\n│   │   └── post2.md\n│   ├── 1-landscape.jpg\n│   ├── 2-sunset.jpg\n│   ├── _index.md\n│   ├── content-1.md\n│   └── content-2.md\n├── 1-logo.png\n└── _index.md\n```\n\nThe file tree above shows three bundles. Note that the home page bundle cannot contain other content pages, although other files (images etc.) are allowed.\n\n## Organization of content source\n\nIn Hugo, your content should be organized in a manner that reflects the rendered website.\n\nWhile Hugo supports content nested at any level, the top levels (i.e.`content/ `) are special in Hugo and are considered the content type used to determine layouts etc. To read more about sections, including how to nest them, see sections.\n\nWithout any additional configuration, the following will automatically work:\n\n```\n.\n└── content\n    └── about\n    |   └── index.md  // \u003c- https://example.org/about/\n    ├── posts\n    |   ├── firstpost.md   // \u003c- https://example.org/posts/firstpost/\n    |   ├── happy\n    |   |   └── ness.md  // \u003c- https://example.org/posts/happy/ness/\n    |   └── secondpost.md  // \u003c- https://example.org/posts/secondpost/\n    └── quote\n        ├── first.md       // \u003c- https://example.org/quote/first/\n        └── second.md      // \u003c- https://example.org/quo
… (output truncated)

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking Hugo documentation to see how it handles underscore-prefixed content files.

Hugo ignores files prefixed with underscores by default. The `__` prefix would be skipped unless configuration changes are made.

Hugo 0.128+ may have changed its default behavior for underscore-prefixed files. The user wants `__` at the start of filenames. Using `draft__` instead would avoid Hugo's default ignore rule. Alternatively, the `ignoreFiles` setting could be adjusted to allow these files.

The underscore prefix rule applies to the first character, so `__filename.md` would still be ignored by Hugo. The user likely wants the prefix purely for visual distinction in their editor.
</details></think>

In fact, `_index.md` is a required file for Hugo to correctly generate section and homepage list pages [2][3].\n\nIf you wish to ignore other specific files, use the `ignoreFiles` setting in your configuration file. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Hugo does not ignore files starting with underscores by default. Using `__draft-name.md` will work fine.

Draft URLs would include the `__` prefix unless slugs are set in frontmatter. The convention is to use `__post-name.md` with `draft: true`, then rename to `post-name.md` and set `draft: false` when publishing.

Adding `slug:` fields to drafts will keep dev preview URLs clean. The plan covers renaming 26 draft files, updating skill documentation, checking archetypes and READMEs, and reviewing template references.

A script will rename all 26 draft files using standard `mv`. The workspace may not be a git repository, so git commands are avoided. The first batch of drafts includes `wifi-router.md`, `password-managers.md`, and several others.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Hugo accepts `__`-prefixed filenames. Renaming draft posts and updating the project convention in the skills/docs.


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog &amp;&amp; for f in *.md; do if grep -q &#39;^draft: true&#39; &#34;$f&#34; 2&gt;/dev/null; then base=&#34;${f%.md}&#34;; if [[ &#34;$base&#34; != __* ]]; then git mv &#34;$f&#34; &#34;__${base}.md&#34; 2&gt;/dev/null || mv &#34;$f&#34; &#34;__${base}.md&#34;; echo &#34;renamed: $f -&gt; __${base}.md&#34;; fi; fi; done</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog && for f in *.md; do if grep -q '^draft: true' "$f" 2>/dev/null; then base="${f%.md}"; if [[ "$base" != __* ]]; then git mv "$f" "__${base}.md" 2>/dev/null || mv "$f" "__${base}.md"; echo "renamed: $f -> __${base}.md"; fi; fi; done
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Adding `slug` fields so dev/preview URLs stay clean after the rename:


_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog &amp;&amp; for f in __*.md; do if ! grep -q &#39;^slug:&#39; &#34;$f&#34;; then slug=&#34;${f#__}&#34;; slug=&#34;${slug%.md}&#34;; awk -v slug=&#34;$slug&#34; &#39;
/^---$/ { if (++n == 1) { print; next } }
n == 1 &amp;&amp; !done &amp;&amp; /^draft: true/ { print &#34;slug: \&#34;&#34; slug &#34;\&#34;&#34;; done=1 }
{ print }
&#39; &#34;$f&#34; &gt; &#34;$f.tmp&#34; &amp;&amp; mv &#34;$f.tmp&#34; &#34;$f&#34; &amp;&amp; echo &#34;added slug to $f -&gt; $slug&#34;; fi; done</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/blog && for f in __*.md; do if ! grep -q '^slug:' "$f"; then slug="${f#__}"; slug="${slug%.md}"; awk -v slug="$slug" '
/^---$/ { if (++n == 1) { print; next } }
n == 1 && !done && /^draft: true/ { print "slug: \"" slug "\""; done=1 }
{ print }
' "$f" > "$f.tmp" && mv "$f.tmp" "$f" && echo "added slug to $f -> $slug"; fi; done
```

**Added `slug`** to each (except `__blank-blog-template.md`, which already had one) so preview URLs stay clean — e.g. `/blog/craft-prompt-framework/` instead of `/blog/__craft-prompt-framework/`.

**Convention going forward:**
- Draft: `__my-post.md` + `draft: true` + `slug: "my-post"`
- Publish: rename to `my-post.md`, set `draft: false`, drop the `slug` line if it matches the filename

This is documented in the jorap-voice and hugo-template-guidance skills so agents follow it when creating new posts.
